title: SIADH - Hyponatremia
chief_complaint: Confusion and lethargy for 2 days. He's been having a persistent cough. He was started on sertraline 2 weeks ago for depression.

hidden_data.cmp: Na 118, K 4.0, Cl 90, HCO3 24, BUN 10, Cr 0.8, Glucose 100

correct_diagnosis: Severe Hyponatremia from SIADH (Sertraline-Induced) complicated by Pneumonia
key_learning_points: Hyponatremia: Na <135. Severe: Na <120. Risk of cerebral edema, seizures, coma
SIADH diagnosis: Hypotonic hyponatremia + euvolemia + urine Osm >100 + urine Na >40 + normal renal, adrenal, thyroid function
Common SIADH causes: Medications (SSRIs, Carbamazepine, Cyclophosphamide), CNS disorders, Pulmonary disease (pneumonia, TB), Malignancy (SCLC)
Treatment: Fluid restriction <1L/day (first-line). Salt tablets. Vasopressin antagonists (Tolvaptan) if refractory. Hypertonic saline (3% NaCl) if severe symptoms
CORRECT SLOWLY: Max 8-10 mEq/L per 24h. Rapid correction → osmotic demyelination syndrome (central pontine myelinolysis). Target 4-6 mEq/L increase in first 24h
